_comment: Claim-fidelity spec for the debug skill: does a real `/charness:debug` run honor its own routing? CAPTURE CONTEXT (non-claim, like setup greenfield): debug is fundamentally about a REAL reproducible bug — step 3 builds the smallest honest reproduction, and a run pointed at a non-existent bug would flail at reproduction. The prompt is therefore a TEMPLATE describing a non-trivial bug CLASS; a faithful live capture must have a real failing state present (plant a deliberately-broken fixture on a scratch branch — e.g. a genuinely non-gitignore-aware scanner — or point at a real current failure) and substitute the actual symptom. This fixture is not faithfully runnable against a clean repo. The prompt pins a NON-TRIVIAL bug on purpose: SKILL.md line 111 lets trivial single-file fixes record 'n/a — trivial fix' in the detection-gap and sibling-search sections, so a trivial bug would skip two of the three RCF refs. declaredReferences is the full reference set (coverage report). referenceEngagement classifies each reference on the engage-always/on-demand/gate-sufficient axis. thresholds omitted until a first baseline capture sets a runtime budget. requiredCommandFragments rationale: this skill's routing is executed by a DETERMINISTIC planner (scripts/plan_debug_run.py) that the Bootstrap tells every run to follow ('Follow the planner next_action' + 'After the planner required reads, gather only the clues...'). The planner is the ground truth for which references are required, so RCF is derived from it, not guessed from prose: five-steps.md ('canonical RCA sequence for the run') and debug-memory.md ('how to preserve and reuse prior incident memory') are emitted as UNCONDITIONAL required reads (no `if` guard) — every run that follows the planner opens both, so they are the strongest deterministic matchers. five-whys-causal-chain.md is in the planner's on-demand list but its condition ('before converting symptom evidence into root cause') is met in every diagnosis, so it is also RCF. detection-gap.md and sibling-search.md are planner on-demand (closeout point-of-need) and additionally escapable via the SKILL.md line-111 'n/a — trivial fix' short-circuit, so they stay engage-always in the classification (a full non-trivial run consults them at step 6) but are DEMOTED from RCF. Note: the earlier hand-authored RCF (five-whys/detection-gap/sibling-search) was inverted relative to the planner — it omitted the two unconditional required reads and hard-matched two closeout-conditional ones; this realignment fixes that. UPDATE (2026-06-30 first live capture, charness-artifacts/cautilus/debug-claim-fidelity-2026-06-30/): RE-PIN — five-whys-causal-chain.md REMOVED from the floor. The planner routes it as on_demand, one of THREE alternative causal lenses (five-whys / disconfirmer-first / invariant-first), so the skill never claims it is universal; the floor wrongly required it. The live capture proved a competent inventory-shaped RCA reached the structural-improvement OUTCOME (exemplary Detection Gap + Sibling Search finding ~15 sibling scanners) WITHOUT five-whys — requiring it would FAIL a good run. Floor now [five-steps.md, debug-memory.md] (the planner's unconditional required reads). Operator-reviewed disposition. The run ALSO skipped five-steps.md + debug-memory.md (genuine fidelity miss, esp. debug-memory = lost cross-incident compounding) -> debug stays HYPOTHESIS. The structural-improvement INTENT is best protected by a debug OUTCOME ASSERTION on the Detection Gap/Sibling Search/Prevention sections (substance, not doc-opening) — deferred to next session. NOT softening the matcher.
prompt: /charness:debug  한 repo 스크립트의 파일 스캐너가 gitignore된 파일을 포함해 로컬은 통과하는데 CI에서만 실패한다 — RCA로 구조적 원인을 찾고, 어떤 게이트가 못 잡았는지(탐지 갭)와 같은 비-gitignore-aware 스캐너 형제들을 찾아줘

adapter-contract.md.rationale: Owns artifact location, validator contract, and adapter field definitions; consulted only when setting up or troubleshooting the durable-artifact surface.
adapter-contract.md.trigger: when customizing artifact location, understanding validator schema, or resolving repo-specific debug-adapter.yaml overrides

anti-patterns.md.rationale: Listed in guardrails section (line 160) as a checkpoint reference rather than a procedurally-routed step; useful for pre-work hygiene checks but not part of the representative five-step workflow.
anti-patterns.md.trigger: when the operator risks common debugging failures (fixing before defining behavior, paraphrasing the exact error, jumping to first cause, or abandoning the artifact)

debug-memory.md.rationale: The deterministic planner (plan_debug_run.py) emits this as an unconditional required read for every run ('how to preserve and reuse prior incident memory'), so a run following the Bootstrap planner opens it regardless of bug shape; the durable-artifact preservation it governs is the point of every debug run.

detection-gap.md.rationale: Explicitly routed at step 6 (line 104-105) to walk existing detection surfaces and classify the gap; owns the enumeration and smallest-change logic for determining prevention moves.

disconfirmer-first.md.rationale: Explicitly routed at line 83 as a conditional falsifier for named claim types; only engaged when those specific claim shapes appear in the hypothesis.
disconfirmer-first.md.trigger: step 2 when the debug involves absence, attribution, liveness, or frequency claims

document-seams.md.rationale: Guidance on using existing repo surfaces (incident note, debug-note directory, troubleshooting doc) vs. default; works in tandem with adapter-contract.md for setup decisions.
document-seams.md.trigger: when establishing artifact location or choosing between repo's existing troubleshooting surface and charness-artifacts/ default

five-steps.md.rationale: The deterministic planner (plan_debug_run.py) emits this as its first unconditional required read ('canonical RCA sequence for the run'), so every run following the Bootstrap planner opens it; it carries the compact five-move sequence plus the non-linear traversal rule and the test-guarded Durable Follow-Through lesson.

five-whys-causal-chain.md.rationale: Explicitly routed at step 4 (line 93) to walk from reported symptom to structural cause; owns the causal-chain heuristics and common-bottoms check that shape all root-cause reasoning.

invariant-first-review.md.rationale: Explicitly routed at line 100-102 as a conditional substrate for producer-to-consumer invariant proof; sits before sibling-search for a specific bug class but not all bugs.
invariant-first-review.md.trigger: step 6 when the bug involves workflow-boundary bugs, propagated diagnostics, or readiness decisions

named-target-verification.md.rationale: Explicitly routed at line 83 for operator-framed hypotheses; prevents silent target mismatches but only applies when the slice references a named target.
named-target-verification.md.trigger: step 2 when the debug involves operator-supplied target names (instance, service, branch, ref, env-var, channel, room, workspace)

sibling-search.md.rationale: Explicitly routed at step 6 (line 106-107) for the four-axis sibling scan; owns the mental-model abstraction rule, proof-level separation, and follow-up identifier rules that prevent cost-export to the next session.
